title: Dracula gorgona
taxonomy: Dracula gorgona is classified within the plant family Orchidaceae. This species is part of the genus Dracula, which includes approximately 50 species of epiphytic orchids. Dracula gorgona, like other members of the genus, is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The genus Dracula is often referred to as the "dracula orchids" due to their unique flower morphology. The classification of Dracula gorgona is further detailed within the subtribe Pleurothallidinae.

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and promote wound healing.
